'''''UFC 302: Makhachev vs. Poirier''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on June 1, 2024, at the [[Prudential Center]], in [[Newark, New Jersey]], United States.<ref name="Newark">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/03/ufc-302-official-newark-new-jersey-june-1-multiple-fights-announced|title=UFC 302 official for Newark on June 1; multiple fights announced|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2024-03-28|access-date=2024-03-28}}</ref>

==Background==
The event marked the promotion's tenth visit to Newark and first since [[UFC 288]] in May 2023.<ref name="Newark" />

The debut of the new UFC Official Fight Gloves took place at this event.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-04-12 |title=Why is the UFC changing their gloves? |url=https://www.dexerto.com/mma/why-is-the-ufc-changing-their-gloves-2644845/ |access-date=2024-04-13 |website=Dexerto |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Sherdog.com |title=UFC Releases New Glove Design, Claiming to Reduce Chance of Injury and Eye Pokes |url=https://www.sherdog.com/news/news/UFC-Releases-New-Glove-Design-Claiming-to-Reduce-Chance-of-Injury-and-Eye-Pokes-193322 |access-date=2024-04-13 |website=Sherdog |language=en}}</ref>

A [[UFC Lightweight Championship]] bout between current champion [[Islam Makhachev]] and former interim champion [[Dustin Poirier]] headlined the event.<ref name="Fights">{{cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/4/14/24129910/islam-makhachev-vs-dustin-poirier-sean-stricklands-return-set-for-ufc-302|title=Islam Makhachev vs. Dustin Poirier, Sean Strickland's return set for UFC 302|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2024-04-14|access-date=2024-04-14}}</ref>

A five-round middleweight bout between former [[UFC Middleweight Championship|UFC Middleweight Champion]] [[Sean Strickland]] and former title challenger [[Paulo Costa]] took place in the co-main event.<ref name="Fights"/>

A heavyweight bout between former [[List of Bellator MMA champions#Heavyweight World Championship|Bellator Heavyweight World Champion]] [[Alexander Volkov (fighter)|Alexander Volkov]] and [[Jailton Almeida]] was expected to take place at this event.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2024-03-25|title=Jailton Almeida books quick turnaround fight vs. Alexander Volkov at UFC 302 on June 1|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/03/ufc-302-jailton-almeida-vs-alexander-volkov-booked-june-1|access-date=2024-03-26|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Simon Samano and Nolan King}}</ref> However, the bout was scrapped for unknown reasons and Volkov was booked for [[UFC on ABC: Whittaker vs. Chimaev]] against former interim [[UFC Heavyweight Championship]] challenger [[Sergei Pavlovich]].<ref name="UFC Saudi">{{Cite web|author=Damon Martin|date=2024-03-28|title=Khamzat Chimaev vs. Robert Whittaker set as main event, 4 more fights announced for UFC Saudi Arabia on June 22|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/3/28/24114291/khamzat-chimaev-vs-robert-whittaker-set-main-event-4-more-fights-announced-ufc-saudi-arabia-june-22|access-date=2024-03-28|publisher=mmafighting.com}}</ref> Almeida faced [[Alexander Romanov (fighter)|Alexander Romanov]] instead.<ref>{{Cite web|publisher=sherdog.com|title=Jailton Almeida vs. Alexander Romanov Booked for UFC 302 on June 1|url=https://www.sherdog.com/news/news/Jailton-Almeida-vs-Alexander-Romanov-Booked-for-UFC-302-on-June-1-193276|date=2024-04-09|access-date=2024-04-10|author=Tristen Critchfield}}</ref>

A women's strawweight bout between former [[Invicta Fighting Championships#Atomweight Championship|Invicta FC Atomweight Champion]] [[Michelle Waterson-Gomez]] and [[Gillian Robertson]] was scheduled for the event.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Jay Anderson|date=2024-03-16|title=Michelle Waterson-Gomez vs. Gillian Robertson booked for UFC 302 in Newark|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2024/03/16/michelle-waterson-gomez-vs-gillian-robertson-booked-for-ufc-302-in-newark/|access-date=2024-03-17|publisher=cagesidepress.com}}</ref> However, the bout was postponed to [[UFC 303]] for unknown reasons.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Julio Fernando Navas|date=2024-04-08|title=Michelle Waterson-Gomez vs. Gillian Robertson is postponed until UFC 303|url=https://www.mmaaldia.com/waterson-vs-robertson-agendada-para-el-ufc-303/|access-date=2024-04-09|publisher=mmaaldia.com}} {{in lang|es}}</ref>

[[Jeremiah Wells]] was expected to face [[Niko Price]] in a welterweight bout.<ref>{{Cite web|publisher=mmaaldia.com|author=Diego Martinez|date=2024-04-04|title=Niko Price vs. Jeremiah Wells added to UFC 302|url=https://www.mmaaldia.com/niko-price-vs-jeremiah-wells-incluido-al-ufc-302/|access-date=2024-04-05}} {{in lang|es}}</ref> However, Wells pulled out due to undisclosed reasons and was replaced by [[Alex Morono]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/4/18/24134237/ufc-302-adds-several-fights-to-islam-makhachev-vs-dustin-poirier-card|title=UFC 302 adds several fights to Islam Makhachev vs. Dustin Poirier card|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2024-04-18|access-date=2024-04-20}}</ref> Price and Morono previously met at [[UFC Fight Night: Bermudez vs. The Korean Zombie]] in February 2017, with the bout originally ending via second-round knockout for Price, before ultimately being overturned to a no contest due to him testing positive for marijuana.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://mmajunkie.com/2017/02/ufc-fight-night-104-results-niko-price-puts-alex-morono-away-with-ko-at-round-2-buzzer|title=UFC Fight Night 104 results: Niko Price puts Alex Morono away with KO at Round 2 buzzer|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Steven Marrocco|date=2017-02-04|access-date=2017-02-04}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2017/3/17/14962180/three-fighters-test-positive-for-marijuana-at-ufc-fight-night-104|title=Three fighters test positive for marijuana at UFC Fight Night 104|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Marc Raimondi|date=2017-03-17|access-date=2024-04-20}}</ref>

[[Su Mudaerji]] and [[Joshua Van]] were expected to meet in a flyweight bout at this event.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://asianmma.com/joshua-van-faces-su-mudaerji-at-ufc-302/|title=Joshua Van faces Su Mudaerji at UFC 302|publisher=asianmma.com|author=Staff|date=2017-04-09|access-date=2024-04-24}}</ref> However, Su pulled out in late April due to undisclosed reasons.<ref name="SuOut">{{Cite news|url=https://www.sherdog.com/news/news/Joshua-Van-Announces-UFC-302-Bout-Against-Su-Mudaerji-Has-Been-Canceled-193418|title=Joshua Van announces UFC 302 bout against Su Mudaerji has been canceled|publisher=sherdog.com|author=Sayan Nag|date=2024-04-22|access-date=2024-04-24}}</ref> He was replaced by [[Tatsuro Taira]].<ref name="Taira in">{{Cite news|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/04/ufc-302-tatsuro-taira-vs-joshua-van-fight-card|title=Tatsuro Taira fills UFC 302 opening, fights Joshua Van|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2024-04-25|access-date=2024-04-25}}</ref> In turn, both fighters were moved to [[UFC on ESPN: Perez vs. Taira]] two weeks later, where Taira faced a different opponent and Van's bout was ultimately cancelled.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Drake Riggs|date=2024-05-13|title=Shake-up! UFC Vegas 93 re-arranges matchups, Alex Perez vs. Tatsuro Taira now headlines|url=https://www.mmamania.com/2024/5/13/24155839/shake-up-ufc-vegas-93-re-arranges-matchups-alex-perez-vs-tatsuro-taira-now-headlines-mma|access-date=2024-05-13|publisher=mmamania.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2024-06-14|title=UFC on ESPN 58 weigh-in results: Ranked flyweight misses mark by 3.5 pounds, fight scrapped|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/06/ufc-espn-58-las-vegas-alex-perez-vs-tatsuro-taira-weigh-in-results-live-stream|access-date=2024-06-14|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff}}</ref>

A middleweight bout between [[Roman Dolidze]] and former [[Legacy Fighting Alliance#LFA Middleweight Championship|LFA Middleweight Champion]] [[Anthony Hernandez]] was scheduled to take place at the event.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/3/25/24112181/anthony-hernandez-vs-roman-dolidze-on-tap-for-ufc-302-in-june|title=Anthony Hernandez vs. Roman Dolidze on tap for UFC 302 in June|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2024-03-25|access-date=2024-03-26}}</ref> However, Hernandez was forced out of the bout due to a torn ligament in his hand.<ref name="Hernandez out">{{Cite news|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/4/29/24144829/anthony-hernandez-out-of-ufc-302-fight-with-roman-dolidze|title=Anthony Hernandez out of UFC 302 fight with Roman Dolidze|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Jed Meshew|date=2024-04-29|access-date=2024-04-30}}</ref>

[[Road to UFC Season 1]] flyweight winner [[Park Hyun-sung]] was expected to face [[André Lima (fighter)|André Lima]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/lists/ufc-news-bellator-pfl-matchup-roundup-fight-announcements-april-8-2024|title=Matchup Roundup: New UFC, PFL, Bellator fights announced in the past week (April 1–7)|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2024-04-08|access-date=2024-05-01}}</ref> However, he pulled out due to a knee injury and was replaced by promotional newcomer Nyamjargal Tumendemberel.<ref name="ParkOut">{{Cite news|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/05/ufc-302-hyunsung-park-knee-injury-andre-lima-nyamjargal-tumendemberel|title=Video: HyunSung Park out of UFC 302 after accident in gym leads to knee ligament rupture|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2024-05-01|access-date=2024-05-01}}</ref> In turn, Tumendemberel was forced to withdraw from the event due to visa issues and was replaced by Mitch Raposo.<ref name="Raposo vs. Lima">{{Cite web|date=2024-05-21|title=Mitch Raposo steps into UFC 302 for debut vs. Andre Lima|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/05/ufc-302-fight-card-mitch-raposo-vs-andre-lima|access-date=2024-05-21|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King}}</ref> At the weigh ins, Lima weighed in at 130 pounds, four pounds over the flyweight non-title fight limit. The bout proceeded at catchweight and he was fined 30 percent of his purse which went to Raposo.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/05/ufc-302-islam-makhachev-vs-dustin-poirier-newark-weigh-in-results-live-video-stream|title=UFC 302 weigh-in results: One fighter misses, but Makhachev and Poirier set for headliner|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff|date=2024-05-31|access-date=2024-05-31}}</ref>

==Bonus awards==
The following fighters received $50,000 bonuses.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Dan Hiergesell|date=2024-06-02|title=Bonuses! Holland banks $50K for breaking an arm|url=https://www.mmamania.com/2024/6/2/24169404/official-bonus-winners-ufc-302-results-espn-ppv-makhachev-poirier-holland|access-date=2024-06-02|publisher=mmamania.com}}</ref>
*'''Fight of the Night: Islam Makhachev vs. Dustin Poirier'''
*'''Performance of the Night: Islam Makhachev''' and '''Kevin Holland'''
